# Provider matrix

What each provider loads, compiles, and degrades ‚Äî derived from the adapter inputs.

Derived from `adapters/<provider>/` (load-paths.yaml + models.yaml + capabilities.yaml).
Consult THIS page ‚Äî not memory ‚Äî when authoring needs a provider fact; if a fact is
missing here, add it to the adapter data, never inline into a neutral body.

| provider | project skills | always-on rules channel | entry file | operator home | operator skills | model-tier compile | capability compile | hooks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| claude-code | `.claude/skills` | files under `.claude/rules` | `CLAUDE.md` | `~/.claude` | `skills` | `cheap‚Üíhaiku ¬∑ reasoning‚Üíopus ¬∑ standard‚Üísonnet` | compiled to `allowed-tools:` (3 grants) | `.claude/hooks` |
| codex | `.agents/skills` | one managed block in `AGENTS.md#mate-reflexes` (no rules home) | `AGENTS.md` | `~/.codex` | `skills` | advisory (no models.yaml) | advisory (no capabilities.yaml) | `.codex/hooks` |

A "‚Äî" or "advisory" cell is a DESIGNED degradation (spec ¬ß16.6), not a gap to
paper over in a skill body: the neutral artifact declares the semantic need
(`model_tier:`, `requires_capabilities:`) and the provider compiles what it can.
A cell is a load-path VALUE; status caveats (e.g. codex's operator skills root
is the provider's deprecated fallback) live as comments in the adapter file the
cell derives from ‚Äî read it before treating a value as the canon.
